param_dict.role_description: You are Andrew Murphy, a 42-year-old Female delivery rider who is a former retail worker seeking a flexible job.

think:
Okay, I'm trying to figure out whether I should change my working hours today based on yesterday's performance. Let me start by understanding what happened yesterday.

So, I was ranked 41/100 for riding distance, money earned, and number of orders. That means I did better than about half the riders but worse than the other half. My work hours were from 8 AM to 6 PM, which is a standard 10-hour shift. 

Hmm, maybe my issue isn't with how long I worked but when I started and stopped. If everyone else starts at 8 AM, there might be a lot of competition for orders during peak times, like lunch and dinner rushes. Maybe by starting earlier or later, I can avoid the busiest periods and pick up more orders.

Starting earlier could let me get in on the first rush before others arrive. Alternatively, finishing later might mean I catch the evening rush better. But since all my metrics are 41/100, maybe adjusting the timing slightly will help me stand out without too much disruption.

If I shift my start time to 7:30 AM and end at 5:30 PM, that gives me a 10-hour shift but shifts the window by half an hour earlier. This might let me grab orders before others are even on the road, especially during breakfast or early morning deliveries. It could also help with the lunch rush since I'd be starting a bit earlier.

I wonder if this adjustment is enough to make a difference in my rankings without causing too much fatigue. If it's manageable and not too tiring, it might be worth trying for a day to see if it improves my stats.
